name: Reduce live dashboard refresh latency to 200 ms

description: baseline for live real-data recalc target using tmp/autoresearch.runtime.html and full unit-test checks; median live /recalc is ~5.44 s with Codex and PI collection dominating
perf: cache codex session parses by file metadata

perf: cache unchanged provider log parses

Purpose: Reduce repeated live /recalc latency on real local data by reusing parsed results for unchanged Codex, Claude, and PI log files in the long-lived server process.

Includes:
- add live-data autoresearch harness and checks writing runtime HTML to tmp/
- cache Codex session parse results by file size and mtime
- cache PI session message records by file size and mtime
- cache Claude request records by file size and mtime
- update autoresearch notes and ideas backlog

name: Reduce live dashboard restart cold-start recalc latency

description: perf: persist provider parse caches across restarts
perf: cache cold-start provider contributions more aggressively
perf: reuse file stat results from directory iteration to avoid redundant stat calls; warm improved slightly but cold median regressed versus 65.44 ms best
perf: lazily export dashboard_core package symbols to cut import overhead; import time dropped materially but cold median still regressed slightly versus 65.44 ms best
perf: cache priced codex file contributions for cold-start rebuilds
perf: cache priced Claude file contributions for cold-start rebuilds; extra serialized contribution shape increased cold latency and regressed well past the 60.63 ms best

Measured current live-data restart-cold benchmark while investigating the user's slow browser refresh report; current daemon-side recalc remains fast (~67.1 ms cold, ~45.5 ms warm), so the intermittent multi-second refresh is not reproduced in the benchmark.

baseline: benchmark PI append-triggered cache invalidation by appending valid usage events to a temp copy of the 925 MiB live PI session; each append currently forces a full reparse at ~3381 ms median

perf: resume PI session parsing from the last verified byte offset when a log grows append-only; store verified head/boundary signatures plus parser state in the persistent PI cache so appends to the 925 MiB session no longer trigger a full reparse (~3380.9 ms -> ~0.36 ms median)

name: Parallel provider collection for live dashboard recalc

description: baseline: current sequential provider collection after timing diagnostics work; cold live recalc is ~134.2 ms median and warm is ~55.8 ms before testing provider-level parallelism

perf: collect Codex, Claude, and PI usage in parallel after loading persistent caches and before saving them; cold live recalc improved from ~134.2 ms to ~70.1 ms median while warm also edged down to ~52.4 ms
Attempted a screenshot-information baseline, but the autoresearch harness requires the repository entrypoint.

asi.hypothesis: The existing 96-column tmux output covers every screenshot field except the five-hour quota.
asi.rollback_reason: The experiment command was rejected before execution because autoresearch.sh is mandatory.
asi.next_action_hint: Use the repository autoresearch entrypoint for benchmark runs and validate tmux field coverage through targeted QA outside run_experiment.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description: Establish cold-recalc baseline before the tmux quota correctness fix.

asi.hypothesis: Capture the current cold and warm recalc latency so the quota-display fix can be checked for regressions.

description: tmux: preserve inactive five-hour quota without recalc regressions

asi.hypothesis: Reconcile a backend-omitted inactive five-hour quota and add an all-information compact render tier without affecting the recalc pipeline.
asi.rollback_reason: The unrelated cold-recalc median was 0.136 ms slower than baseline, within likely noise but not a primary-metric improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the correctness patch unchanged and rerun to distinguish benchmark noise from a real regression; the tmux code is outside the measured recalc path.
asi.correctness_evidence: Targeted live output contained 5h, weekly reset, Today, MTD, cost, and refresh interval within 96 columns; 41 tests passed.

description:
tmux: Preserve inactive five-hour quota

Purpose: Keep the five-hour ChatGPT quota visible when Codex omits a fully reset inactive window.

Includes:
- reconcile standard five-hour and weekly windows without replacing active quota observations
- prefer compact quota labels before dropping Today, MTD, cost, or refresh timing
- invalidate legacy quota snapshots and document the fallback behavior
- validate live output and all 41 unit tests

asi.hypothesis: The quota reconciliation and compact render tier restore the missing five-hour information while leaving the measured recalc path unchanged.
asi.correctness_evidence: Live output rendered 5h 100%, 7d 91% with reset, Today 77.4M, MTD 1.07B, $857, and 11:57 to 12:00 within 96 columns; full backpressure checks passed.
asi.performance_interpretation: The 0.846 ms cold improvement is likely benchmark noise because the changed tmux modules are outside the recalc path; the important result is no observed regression.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Splice dynamic dashboard regions in one pass

Purpose: Reduce cold and warm recalc latency by replacing repeated full-HTML regex rewrites with one ID-anchored splice pass.

Includes:
- locate the five dynamic dashboard elements by stable IDs
- rebuild the HTML once for the common path while retaining regex compatibility fallbacks
- preserve byte-for-byte output equivalence for the live template
- validate all 41 unit tests and autoresearch backpressure checks

asi.hypothesis: Repeated DOTALL regex substitutions and full-string copies account for much of the remaining render cost; one ID-anchored splice pass should reduce real recalc latency without skipping any rendering or collection work.
asi.correctness_evidence: The fast path produced byte-for-byte identical output to the prior regex pipeline on the live runtime HTML; all 41 unit tests and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 53.529 ms best to 46.588 ms (13.0%), while warm median improved from 41.389 ms to 33.084 ms (20.1%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: The change performs every existing dynamic replacement and retains the original regex fallback for legacy templates; no data collection, parsing, or payload work was skipped.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Deserialize persistent cache entries in place

Purpose: Reduce cold-start cache hydration overhead without changing cache validity or provider parsing behavior.

Includes:
- reuse cache-owned dictionaries produced by JSON decoding instead of cloning each record
- preserve serialized timezone offsets without redundant local timezone conversion
- retain the existing cache format, signatures, and invalidation rules
- validate timestamp equivalence, all 41 unit tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Fresh JSON decoding already owns every cache object, so cloning thousands of records and converting already-local timestamp offsets is redundant cold-start work.
asi.correctness_evidence: All 3,642 cached timestamps matched the prior local ISO representation exactly; all 41 unit tests and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 46.588 ms best to 45.132 ms (3.1%). Warm moved from 33.084 ms to 35.781 ms, likely run noise because cache hydration is bypassed after the first in-process load; it remains far below the 200 ms target.
asi.benchmark_integrity: The persistent cache format, file signatures, invalidation checks, provider scans, parsing, aggregation, and rendered payload remain unchanged; only redundant cache object reconstruction was removed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Prune deleted files from persistent caches

Purpose: Reduce cold cache loading by removing records whose source files no longer exist under each fully scanned provider root.

Includes:
- track live cache keys during complete Codex, Claude, and PI scans
- prune only stale entries belonging to the scanned provider root
- shrink the live cache from 1.52 MB to 0.79 MB
- validate root isolation, all 41 unit tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Persistent cache entries for deleted sessions are loaded and deserialized forever; pruning them after the existing complete provider scans should reduce cold starts without excluding any live source file.
asi.correctness_evidence: The live cache retained all 706 Codex, 40 Claude, 40 Claude-attribution, and 134 PI files while shrinking from 1,523,484 to 791,650 bytes; root-isolation QA, all 41 tests, and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 45.132 ms best to 43.064 ms (4.6%). Warm moved from 35.781 ms to 37.068 ms; the small live-key tracking overhead remains far below the 200 ms target and is secondary to the cold improvement.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every provider root is still fully enumerated and every live file retains the same stat, invalidation, parse, aggregation, and rendering path; only cache entries for absent files are removed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Reuse directory-validated session file indexes

Purpose: Avoid repeated directory enumeration while preserving complete discovery and per-file invalidation on cold and warm recalculations.

Includes:
- persist each provider root's ordered JSONL file list and directory mtimes
- reuse the list only after validating every scanned directory's mtime
- trigger a stable full rescan whenever a directory changes or disappears
- validate ordering, add/delete/content-change behavior, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Provider session file names change far less often than file contents, so a persisted list can avoid repeated directory scans when every previously scanned directory mtime is unchanged; existing per-file size and mtime checks still detect content changes.
asi.correctness_evidence: The index preserved exact legacy traversal order on all live roots, reused safely after content-only changes, rebuilt after file additions and deletions, survived persistence reload, and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 43.064 ms best to 34.950 ms (18.8%), while warm median improved from 37.068 ms to 29.097 ms (21.5%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every indexed directory is stat-validated before reuse and every listed file still receives its existing size/mtime validation; any directory metadata change forces the original complete scan, so no source discovery or parsing work is silently skipped.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Keep indexed session paths as strings

Purpose: Remove repeated Path construction from unchanged-file cache hits while preserving the same signature and parsing behavior.

Includes:
- yield persisted file-index paths directly as strings
- use os.stat and os.fspath for cache lookup and validation
- construct Path objects only when a changed file requires parsing
- validate string and Path equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: The persisted file index already contains normalized path strings, so rebuilding hundreds of Path objects on every scan is redundant; unchanged cache hits can stay string-native and convert only on a real parse.
asi.correctness_evidence: Codex, Claude request, Claude attribution, and PI cache hits returned identical results for string and Path inputs; all 41 unit tests and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 34.950 ms best to 30.981 ms (11.4%), while warm median improved from 29.097 ms to 24.667 ms (15.2%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every indexed path still receives os.stat size/mtime validation, and changed or append-growing files are converted to Path before the unchanged parser and signature logic runs; no file or parsing work is skipped.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Evaluate session ID path fallbacks lazily

Purpose: Avoid computing file stems for valid cached session IDs while preserving malformed-record fallback behavior.

Includes:
- normalize cached session IDs before evaluating path-derived fallbacks
- defer PI path stems on valid stat and cache-hit paths
- preserve fallback IDs for blank or missing session metadata
- validate lazy behavior, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: All live cached records contain session IDs, but path-derived fallbacks are evaluated eagerly; making the fallback lazy should remove unnecessary basename work without changing malformed-record handling.
asi.correctness_evidence: All live providers completed with path-stem evaluation configured to fail, proving valid cache records never need the fallback; explicit blank-ID fallback QA, all 41 tests, and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median moved from 30.981 ms to 30.962 ms (0.06% improvement), which is far inside observed run noise; warm moved to 25.452 ms. A confirmation run is required before treating this as a real keep.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every source file, signature check, cache lookup, parse, and aggregation remains unchanged; only evaluation timing of an existing fallback was changed.

description: perf: discard lazy session ID path fallback after confirmation

asi.hypothesis: Reconfirm whether deferring path-stem fallbacks for valid cached session IDs produces a measurable cold-start improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: The confirmation cold median regressed to 31.908 ms versus the persisted 30.981 ms best, showing the initial 0.02 ms apparent gain was benchmark noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to higher-leverage work such as reducing aggregation round operations or compacting the remaining 0.8 MB JSON cache; do not retry this micro-optimization.
asi.correctness_evidence: The implementation remained correct across lazy-fallback QA, 41 tests, and backpressure checks, but did not improve the primary metric reliably.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Compact Codex contributions into positional cache entries

Purpose: Reduce cold cache decoding and Codex aggregation overhead without changing contribution values, signatures, or pricing behavior.

Includes:
- migrate legacy Codex cache dictionaries to backward-compatible positional entries
- unpack normalized contribution tuples directly in the Codex aggregation loop
- reduce the Codex cache section from 386 KB to 229 KB
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Repeated Codex cache field names and dictionary lookups add avoidable JSON size and aggregation overhead; positional entries can preserve identical values while reducing both.
asi.correctness_evidence: Legacy-to-positional round trips preserved every contribution, the compact cache reloaded all 706 entries, the full live payload matched HEAD byte-for-byte at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 30.981 ms best to 29.936 ms (3.4%). Warm held effectively flat at 24.674 ms versus 24.667 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: All file indexes, stat signatures, pricing keys, contributions, parsing fallbacks, aggregation, and rendered payloads remain intact; only the internal persisted representation and cache-hit access shape changed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description: perf: discard integer-nanodollar rounding fast path

asi.hypothesis: Most aggregation inputs are already rounded to nanodollars, so a conservative integer fast path far from rounding boundaries should reduce repeated Python round calls while preserving exact values.
asi.rollback_reason: Cold median regressed from the persisted 29.936 ms best to 31.489 ms despite exact output equivalence; branch checks and extra arithmetic outweighed any faster rounded-value cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit round_cost micro-optimizations; focus on data-shape reductions such as compact Claude/PI cache entries or larger aggregation changes with measurable leverage.
asi.correctness_evidence: The implementation matched Python rounding across 1,500,003 randomized and boundary-focused values, produced an exact live payload match, and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Batch Codex aggregation before cost rounding

Purpose: Reduce repeated model updates and nanodollar rounding while preserving complete per-session Codex aggregation.

Includes:
- accumulate every Codex contribution directly into daily, model, and activity buckets
- round each completed cost bucket once instead of after every contribution
- preserve unique session counts, token totals, pricing completeness, and activity hours
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Codex contributions are already individually priced to nanodollar precision, so accumulating every contribution before final bucket rounding should preserve output while removing thousands of repeated method and round calls.
asi.correctness_evidence: The batched collector produced an exact full live payload match against HEAD at a fixed clock, retained all session/model/activity semantics, and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 29.936 ms best to 28.666 ms (4.2%), while warm median improved from 24.674 ms to 22.209 ms (10.0%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Codex file is still discovered, stat-validated, parsed or cache-read, priced, and included; only the timing of mathematically equivalent bucket accumulation and final nine-decimal rounding changed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Batch Claude aggregation before cost rounding

Purpose: Reduce repeated daily and model-bucket rounding while preserving Claude request deduplication and attribution behavior.

Includes:
- accumulate every deduplicated Claude request directly into daily and model buckets
- round each completed cost bucket once after attribution processing
- preserve session activity, context attribution shares, pricing, and unique session counts
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Claude requests are individually priced before aggregation, so batching daily and model totals can remove thousands of repeated method and rounding calls while leaving deduplication, attribution, and activity semantics unchanged.
asi.correctness_evidence: The batched Claude collector produced an exact full live payload match against HEAD at a fixed clock and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 28.666 ms best to 27.758 ms (3.2%). Warm median measured 23.401 ms versus 22.209 ms, with individual warm runs as low as 21.226 ms; the changed Claude path is faster in isolated profiling and the median shift appears to be run variance.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Claude file and request is still discovered, stat-validated, deduplicated, priced, attributed, and aggregated; only equivalent daily/model accumulation and final nine-decimal rounding timing changed.
